title: agrostis spica-venti subsp. interrupta
intro: Agrostis spica-venti subsp. interrupta, also known as interrupted bent grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Agrostis spica-venti subsp. interrupta include the ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as reduce swelling and pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas or infusions, or used in tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
